summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic core of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker, plus the shared ColorValue foundation) closing GitHub tasks #297, #298, and #299 in a single combined PR.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented with 4113+ tests, PR #698 open and CI-green through 13 rounds of Codex review (55 findings addressed). Codex 👍 still pending after pass 13 fix; PR cannot merge until approval.

repo: no_std float math is done via core_maths::CoreFloat using fully-qualified form (e.g. CoreFloat::powf(x, exp)); CoreFloat lacks min/max — private fmin/fmax helpers are required.
MessageFn only accepts closure signatures registered via manual From<F> impls in crates/ars-core/src/message_fn.rs; no generic fallback exists. Add a new impl for each distinct fn signature.

Drag-machine pattern: adapter drives DragMove/DragEnd from pointer listeners; core only transitions Idle↔Dragging and applies position. on_change_end fires via a typed Effect enum variant on DragEnd, not from within core.
cargo xci-fast is the mandatory pre-push gate (10 checks). The snapshot-count gate requires exactly one insta snapshot per declared state variant — missing a state causes a hard failure.

ars-components has a 90% line AND 90% branch coverage threshold enforced by cargo xtask coverage check-all. Branch coverage requires nightly: cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace --lcov.

Bindable::get() returns the controlled prop value; pending() (added this session) returns the internal/pending value set during drag. Display attrs (aria-valuenow, hidden input, thumb background) must use pending() in controlled mode so they follow in-flight changes.

Hue-bearing channels in ColorValue::new normalize hue into [0, 360) — 360° wraps to 0. Linear controls (ColorSlider, ColorWheel, ColorArea) must track an unwrapped channel value (slider_value / hue_value / x_value / y_value) separately to keep the 360° endpoint reachable in ARIA.

spec validate checks 340 files (components + adapters) and is a required post-edit step. The spec-compile-snippets xci-fast gate compiles spec code blocks in isolation; spec drift from non-existent APIs breaks it.

ColorField needs two separate invalid fields: ctx.invalid (external prop, owned by init/SetProps/SetInvalid) and ctx.parse_error (parser-derived, cleared by value-setting events). Merging them causes SyncValue to silently clear externally-set invalid state.
f64::clamp panics if min > max or either bound is NaN (AngleSlider props). Always use a panic-safe wrapper for user-supplied bounds.

RTL horizontal controls must invert both the rendered position percentage (100.0 - pct) AND the pointer drag normalized position (1.0 - pos) AND the track gradient direction (to left). Forgetting the pointer inversion leaves drag semantics LTR while display is RTL.

code.finding: from_hex byte-slices &hex[0..2] before checking char boundaries — panics on non-ASCII input like '#ああ'. Guard with hex.is_ascii() early.
ColorValue::new only debug_asserts finiteness; release builds propagate NaN/inf into downstream math. Coerce non-finite components to 0.0 before wrap/clamp.
parse_hsb_args feeds out-of-range S/B values into HSB→HSL math before clamping, producing wrong colors for inputs like hsb(0,200%,100%). Clamp to 0..=1 first.

Four new From<F> impls were added for color component closure signatures: Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,f64,&Locale), Fn(f64,f64,&str,&str,&Locale). Each new signature must be registered manually.
RTL pointer inversion must happen in apply_slider_position (1.0 - normalized_pos) for horizontal RTL; only mirroring the rendered pct leaves drag semantics LTR.

SetProps sets ctx.invalid=props.invalid, clobbering parser-derived invalid while input_text is still invalid. Fix: split into ctx.invalid (external) and ctx.parse_error (parser-derived); SyncValue/SetValue clear only parse_error.
f64::clamp panics on min>max or NaN bounds from custom Props. Replace all clamp() calls with a clamp_to_range() helper that guards non-finite/inverted bounds.
In idle state, focused_index is None and every item gets tabindex=-1, making the control unreachable by keyboard. Gate tabindex on tabbable_index() = selected item or first item (tabindex=0) in idle.
Pre-existing map_or_else(…, identity) triggers clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else denied workspace-wide. Stable 1.96 surfaced this; required a 1-line fix before CI could pass.

workflow: Spec-first: spec code blocks must be reconciled to the implementation in the same PR; the spec-compile-snippets gate enforces that blocks compile. Delegate mechanical mirroring to a long-running spec-sync subagent after each code batch.

waiting-for-codex-review skill: post @codex review, poll for 👀 (reviewing) then 👍/threads (verdict). Reply to each thread with the fix + test name and resolve before re-triggering. One @codex review trigger per push.
TDD every Codex finding: write a failing test before implementing the fix. This makes the thread reply concrete and prevents regressions.

Measure branch coverage with the exact CI command (cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace --lcov then cargo xtask coverage check-all) rather than -p, because cross-crate tests lift per-crate coverage ~1%.
Avoid 'status' as a bash variable name in zsh — it is read-only and crashes the script silently.
Spec-sync subagents can be kept alive across multiple rounds via SendMessage rather than spawning a new agent each time, preserving codebase context.

friction: core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max, requiring private fmin/fmax helpers even though the dep was added specifically for no_std float math.
Every new MessageFn closure shape requires a manual From<F> impl in message_fn.rs — not discovered until compile time, requiring a mid-implementation detour.

Extensive spec drift: the specs described non-existent APIs (use_platform_effects, track_pointer_drag, cb.call string-effects, generate_id) that had to be reconciled in both the implementation and the spec docs.
Branch coverage (90% threshold) is only measurable with nightly toolchain; the default build gives no branch signal, so the Coverage CI failure was a surprise after passing all local checks.
A pre-existing clippy lint on pagination/mod.rs (map_or_else with identity, denied workspace-wide) was surfaced by stable 1.96 and had to be fixed before CI could pass — unrelated to the task.
Codex was initially rate-limited ('reached your Codex usage limits') on the first two @codex review triggers, stalling the workflow for an unknown quota-reset duration.
AngleSlider snapshot-count gate failure: xci-fast requires 1 snapshot per state variant; the 3-state machine had only 2 snapshots, caught only after commit/push.

13 rounds of Codex review (55 findings) were needed, many of which were cascading consequences of the same root causes (controlled-mode pending/display consistency, RTL mirroring completeness). The review surface kept widening as fixes exposed adjacent gaps.
The spec-sync subagent occasionally introduced cosmetic formatting changes (multi-line YAML arrays, table alignment) that triggered IDE markdownlint warnings (MD060), requiring post-hoc cleanup.
zsh read-only 'status' variable crashed the first Codex watcher script mid-poll, requiring a rewrite with a renamed variable.
open_items: Codex 👍 still pending after pass 13 fix (commit 9878f4dc pushed, watcher will notify on pass 14 verdict). PR #698 cannot merge until approval + user go-ahead.
PR #698 merge is the user's decision (auto-closes #297/#298/#299 on merge via keywords in PR body).
Adapter specs (leptos/dioxus-components/specialized/color-*) describe future adapter implementation — explicitly out of scope for #297–299.

ColorValue spec location: currently defined in color-picker.md §Types, referenced by 8 components. Could be promoted to spec/shared/color-types.md (would touch manifest + 8 frontmatters) — deferred as orthogonal to implementation correctness.

The MessageFn From-impl listing in spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md does not include the new color component signatures (Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), etc.) — left unlisted for consistency since other color signatures are also absent.
Pre-existing transitive-dep future-incompat note for block v0.1.6 in the workspace — not introduced by this PR but visible in clippy output.
